subject: Biology
title: Red Blood Cells: Oxygen Taxis
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Red Blood Cells: Oxygen Taxis.

hook.text: Red blood cells carry oxygen from your lungs to every tissue in your body.

buildup.text: They're packed with hemoglobin, a protein that binds oxygen and gives blood its color.

discovery.text: Mature red blood cells eject their nucleus to make room for more hemoglobin.

twist.text: Your body makes about 2 million red blood cells every second to replace worn-out ones.

climax.text: Each red blood cell circulates for about 120 days before the spleen recycles it.

punchline.text: No nucleus, no rest—just delivering oxygen.

quiz.question: Why do mature red blood cells lack a nucleus?
options: To make room for more hemoglobin
